new_classes.label_de: Wein mit geschützter geografischer Angabe (g.g.A.) – Qualitätseigenschaft
new_classes.definition_en: Quality borne by a wine that has been granted a protected geographical indication (PGI / g.g.A.) in accordance with EU wine regulations and additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22 of the CN.

new_classes.label_en: PGI wine in containers holding ≤ 2 l (CN 2204 21 80)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit geschützter geografischer Angabe (g.g.A.) in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von 2 l oder weniger (KN 2204 21 80)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes with a protected geographical indication (PGI / g.g.A.), presented in containers with a nominal volume of not more than 2 litres, classified under CN subheading 2204 21 80. Covers both red/rosé and white PGI wines. See additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation: The core distinguishing criteria are fully captured: (1) wine of fresh grapes (producedBy WineFreshGrapesProduction, parent class WineFreshGrapesGrapeMust2204), (2) presented in containers ≤ 2 l (hasPart some SmallContainer), (3) bearing a protected geographical indication — PGI / g.g.A. (has_quality some PGIWineQuality), (4) NOT a PDO wine (complement of PDOWineQuality), (5) NOT sparkling or semi-sparkling (complement of SparklingWineQuality and SemiSparklingWineQuality). A 0.15 gap remains because additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22 contains detailed quantitative conditions (e.g. actual alcoholic strength thresholds, total dry extract thresholds) that define what qualifies as a PGI wine under EU law; these sub-criteria of note 6(a) are not individually modelled here, and the specific sibling subheadings 22042179 (variety/vintage PGI) vs 22042180 (other PGI) distinction — which depends on whether the wine also carries a specific grape variety or vintage year indication — is referenced in the legal notes but not fully axiomatised due to the absence of a variety/vintage quality class in the current TBox.
